key_insight: A single rollout per prompt can still support token-level credit assignment by training a critic to predict Pass@k success probabilities from Monte Carlo outcomes, avoiding expensive multi-sample trajectory comparisons.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more sample-efficient RL training signal for language models in reasoning tasks, with stable optimization and improved high-k success on math benchmarks.
method: Use one rollout per prompt instead of repeated sampling across heterogeneous reasoning traces.
Train a calibrated token-level critic to estimate Pass@k from the prompt prefix via Monte Carlo outcomes.
Use the critic’s prefix-level success probability as a selective advantage signal, emphasizing hard prefixes over easily solved ones.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable learning and consistent gains in Pass@128 on mathematical reasoning benchmarks including HMMT26 and AIME24.
watch_item: Pass@k-based credit assignment may depend on calibration quality and the Pass@k target choice, so poor critic calibration could weaken token-level learning signals.
paper_title: Learning with a Single Rollout via Monte Carlo Pass@k Critic
